questions.question: The Central Vigilance Commission submits an annual report on its activities to:

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions

answer.label: B
answer.text: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions

questions.explanation: The Central Vigilance Commission submits an annual report on its activities to the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions.

questions.question: Central Vigilance Commission (CVC) was established in which year?
questions.explanation: The Central Vigilance Commission (CVC) was established in the year 1964. It was established on the recommendation of the Santhanam Committee on Prevention of Corruption (1962–64).

questions.question: In which year the Central Vigilance Commission (CVC) was given statutory status?
questions.explanation: Initially the Central Vigilance Commission was neither a constitutional body nor a statutory body. In the year 2003, the Parliament enacted a law conferring statutory status on the CVC.

questions.question: The Central Vigilance Commission (CVC) can consist of maximum how many members including the chairman?
questions.explanation: The Central Vigilance Commission (CVC) is a multi-member body. It consists of a Central Vigilance Commissioner who is the chairperson and not more than two vigilance commissioners.

questions.question: Who appoints the Central Vigilance Commissioner?
questions.explanation: The Central Vigilance Commissioner appointed by the president of India by warrant under his hand and seal on the recommendation of a three member committee consisting of the prime minister as its head.
